summary: Top-level /templates/ directory is a stale, unused duplicate of agentteams/templates/ (only 9 constitutional rules, Workflows 1-8) with zero readers anywhere in the pipeline; risks a contributor editing the wrong copy with no effect.
proposed_touch_points: /templates/ (delete or clearly mark legacy); consider a CI check asserting only agentteams/templates/ is read by the pipeline

summary: 3 of 4 universal *.csv.template static files (adjacent-repos-changelog, adjacent-repos-coordination-log, security-decisions.log) have zero readers anywhere in agentteams/**/*.py; the real mechanism is liaison_logs.py's Python constants, creating a duplicated-truth risk if the two ever diverge.

agentteams --dry-run --json does not emit a single parseable JSON document on stdout as --help implies: it prints ~9 lines of human-readable progress text to stdout FIRST, then the JSON payload after — the combined stream is not valid JSON even though the JSON object itself is well-formed once isolated.

A successful --update --merge run's console summary ('No structural or content changes detected...Written: 0 file(s)') can be misleading: confirmed real template-driven content changes were written to disk despite this message, and the printed file count did not match the dry-run's own plan count for the same inputs.

examples/*/expected/ golden snapshots silently drift from the template library over time with no CI check or regeneration reminder; --update --merge alone cannot restore fresh-render parity once pre-existing shrink-guard-suppressed fences have diverged, only --overwrite can, which is security-gated.
proposed_touch_points: tests/test_integration.py::test_snapshot_comparison; a periodic/CI snapshot-freshness check; docs_src/contributing.md regeneration recipe

summary: Two distinct security gates (destructive-action overwrite gate and security-intelligence-freshness gate) both print near-identical 'Security gate blocked ...:' messages, making it easy for an operator to misdiagnose which gate fired and what actually unblocks it.

agentteams --update --merge never re-plans the instructions file (copilot-instructions.md / CLAUDE.md-equivalent) for an existing team, regardless of framework -- only fresh generation includes it in the effective output plan. Confirmed two ways: (1) .github/copilot-instructions.md's mtime was untouched by today's --self --update --merge run despite unrelated fenced content elsewhere changing; (2) a --dry-run --json plan for --framework claude --output .claude/agents --update --merge listed 46 entries and none was '../CLAUDE.md', even though the original from-scratch build-log.json's output_files_map explicitly includes it. Any project relying on --update --merge to keep its instructions file current (not just its per-agent files) will silently drift -- exactly the kind of staleness this session had to hand-fix in both .github/agents/orchestrator.agent.md's and .claude/CLAUDE.md's Constitutional Rules sections.
proposed_touch_points: agentteams/cli/generate.py or agentteams/render.py (update-mode file planning); output_plan.py

summary: references/bridges/<pair>/bridge-manifest.json embeds an absolute source_dir path (confirmed pre-existing via git history in a real consumer repo, predating this session), the same class of finding already fixed twice this session (cli/artifacts.py delivery-receipt; bridge_subagents.py/_goose.py stub bodies). Not fixed here -- flagging as a 4th, not-yet-investigated location for a future pass over agentteams/bridge.py's manifest/report generation to check for the same pattern.

CopilotVSCodeAdapter.vscode_tasks_rel_path() hardcodes "../../.vscode/tasks.json", assuming the agents-dir is always exactly 2 path segments below the true project root (matching the real .github/agents case). When --output points directly at a shallower directory (e.g. this session's own golden-snapshot regen using --output examples/<name>/expected, only 1 segment below its own conceptual project root), the hardcoded offset climbs one level too far and silently writes tasks.json outside the intended output tree entirely (observed: examples/.vscode/tasks.json, sibling to all three example projects, not nested under any one of them). Not fixed here -- discovered incidentally while regenerating example snapshots for BH-11 (blocked nothing, since test_snapshot_comparison only reads *.md/*.svg inside expected/ via rglob); the stray directory was deleted before commit. Same general class as the two absolute-path leaks already fixed this session, but a relative-traversal escape rather than an absolute-path embed.
proposed_touch_points: agentteams/frameworks/copilot_vscode.py (vscode_tasks_rel_path); agentteams/vscode_tasks.py (path resolution/join site) -- consider deriving the offset from the actual agents-dir depth rather than a hardcoded constant

summary: retrospective-remediation.reference.md's self-referential-exception clause justifies routing remediation items to the top-level CSV by citing the dogfood tree as "gitignored... build artifact," naming only .github/agents/. It is silent on .claude/agents/ -- a second dogfood tree (added 2026-07-21) that is actually git-tracked for an unrelated reason (Claude Code needs it locally resolvable). Found by @conflict-auditor during the BH-11 close-out. Practical behavior is still correct (the generic stub stays an empty header-only file regardless) -- just a stale rationale needing a one-line update, not a functional bug.

.claude/CLAUDE.md's Authority Hierarchy and Source Repositories sections cite bare 'templates/' and a nonexistent 'src/' (renamed to agentteams/ in commit 5f6c923); found during audit of the Goose CLI capabilities reference work — the ambiguity makes it plausible a contributor edits the confirmed-stale root-level templates/ directory (see prior 2026-07-22 entry) instead of agentteams/templates/, since the authority doc itself never disambiguates.

security.template.md's Mandatory Review Triggers table (the mechanically-gating table, inside the security_rules_invariant Invariant Core fence) has no dedicated row for software installation (brew/apt/dnf/etc.) or privileged sudo commands. This gap became directly relevant this session: the new cli-tool-discovery.reference.template.md now explicitly tells agents they may install missing tools via a package manager, but the trigger table that determines what @security must review before an action proceeds doesn't cover that action class -- only Rule S-4's general destructive-operation prose does, which is weaker than a table row. Not fixed here per this session's own plan (editing Invariant Core content was ruled out of scope); the new reference doc names the gap honestly rather than overclaiming coverage (caught by @conflict-auditor close-out review).
status: inside security_rules_invariant fence -- requires a deliberate version bump

summary: Rule S-9's criterion 5 (Pathway Safety Verification, security.template.md) was narrowed today to 'privileged or stateful' external interaction, explicitly excluding routine read-only public-data fetches (the fix for a real conflict with the goose-bridge-entry-actionability fix). @security's re-review flagged one remaining ambiguity this narrowing doesn't resolve: a read-only GET with no write and no persisted session but a real-world cost side effect (e.g. a metered/paid API call) is neither clearly 'privileged/stateful' nor clearly a free inert 'routine public fetch' under the current wording. Not a security hole (no credential/data exposure), but a cost-control gap outside Rule S-9's current stated purpose. Not fixed here -- flagged as a non-blocking follow-up by @security during close-out.
status: inside security_rules_invariant fence -- additive wording only
column7: matching today's established pattern for this fence)

summary: OPSEC: .claude/agents/references/memory-index.json (tracked, already committed/pushed in prior history) contains hundreds of absolute filesystem paths with the local OS username, referencing paths across multiple unrelated projects (shared-data, shared-libs, other repos under githubrepositories), not just this project's own path. This project's own path prefix was sanitized in this session's fix-forward commit (1937cbc); the remaining cross-project path variants were not, since a blunt find-replace risks corrupting JSON structure across many differently-shaped absolute paths and deserves a careful, dedicated pass rather than a rushed sed. Git history in this and at least two other repos (researchteam, OrthodoxLLM) retains the original unsanitized values regardless of any working-copy fix -- a full remediation would require history rewrite (filter-repo/BFG), a separate, explicit, user-authorized decision given it rewrites already-pushed public history.
proposed_touch_points: .claude/agents/references/memory-index.json (whatever internal tool/process generates and updates this file -- not found in agentteams/*.py's own source, likely a separate Claude-Code-specific memory/session-index mechanism); git history on affected branches if a rewrite is ever authorized
